title: Java Regex
content:
### Regular Expressions in Java

Regular expressions (regex) are patterns used for matching character sequences in strings.

---

### The Pattern and Matcher Classes

```java
import java.util.regex.*;

Pattern pattern = Pattern.compile("regex");
Matcher matcher = pattern.matcher("input string");
```

---

### Common Regex Patterns

| Pattern | Matches |
|---------|---------|
| `.` | Any character |
| `\d` | Digit (0-9) |
| `\D` | Non-digit |
| `\w` | Word character (a-z, A-Z, 0-9, _) |
| `\W` | Non-word character |
| `\s` | Whitespace |
| `\S` | Non-whitespace |
| `^` | Start of line |
| `$` | End of line |
| `[abc]` | Any of a, b, c |
| `[^abc]` | Not a, b, c |
| `[a-z]` | Range a to z |

---

### Quantifiers

| Quantifier | Description |
|------------|-------------|
| `*` | Zero or more |
| `+` | One or more |
| `?` | Zero or one |
| `{n}` | Exactly n times |
| `{n,}` | At least n times |
| `{n,m}` | Between n and m times |

---

### Matcher Methods

| Method | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `matches()` | Full string matches pattern |
| `find()` | Find next match in string |
| `group()` | Return matched text |
| `start()` | Start index of match |
| `end()` | End index of match |
| `replaceAll(replacement)` | Replace all matches |

---

### String Regex Methods

```java
"Hello123".matches(".*\\d+")       // true
"a,b,c".split(",")                 // ["a", "b", "c"]
"Hello123".replaceAll("\\d", "#") // "Hello###"
```

> **Key Point:** In Java strings, backslashes must be escaped: `\d` becomes `\\d`. Use `Pattern.compile()` for reusable patterns. Precompile patterns for performance in loops.
